reasoning: The FRED series data from 2026-01-16 to 2026-03-13 shows eight consecutive weekly increases in the ANFCI from -0.588 to -0.470, with sequentially accelerating or stable weekly changes (+0.001, +0.006, +0.012, +0.015, +0.019, +0.020, +0.023, +0.022). This strongly indicates a clear and consistent tightening trend in financial conditions. Financial condition indices are heavily smoothed and strongly autocorrelated, meaning established trends are likely to persist in the very short term (one week ahead). Although the long-term baseline (August 2025 - March 2026) linear trend gave a statistical estimate of 0.25 (and a combined estimate of 0.46 when factoring in seasonal priors), the incredibly strong, unbroken short-term momentum provides significant evidence that the series will continue its upward trajectory for at least one more week. I am adjusting the baseline estimate up to 0.65 to reflect this powerful local autoregressive trend.
